# Gap Assessment: iso42001

**Generated at (UTC):** 2026-04-19T18:39:58Z
**Generated by:** gap-assessment/0.2.0
**Scope boundary:** Clinical decision-support and claims-operations AI systems deployed at Contoso Health Insurance. Excludes corporate IT and HR-facing AI systems.
**Reviewed by:** AI Governance Lead, Contoso Health Insurance

## Summary

- Target framework: iso42001
- Total targets: 38
- Coverage score: 15.3%
- Classification counts: covered=5, partially-covered=1, not-covered=30, not-applicable=2
- Targets with warnings: 30

- ISO/IEC 42001:2023, Clause 6.1.2
- ISO/IEC 42001:2023, Clause 6.1.3

## Cross-framework gap visibility

Gaps below are sourced from crosswalk-matrix-builder no-mapping entries. See the crosswalk plugin for the full row list and citations.

### colorado-sb-205 (2 gap rows)

- direction: reference-beyond-target (source: colorado-sb-205, target: iso42001); gap_count: 2
  - Section 6-1-1706: AG exclusive enforcement; no private right of action. ISO 42001 is a voluntary management system standard with no public enforcement regime; Colorado enforcement allocation has no standard analog. [Colorado SB 205]
  - Section 6-1-1703(6): Small-deployer exemption (<50 FTE, non-trainer). ISO 42001 applies regardless of deployer size; no size-based exemption exists. [Colorado SB 205]
- direction: target-beyond-reference (source: iso42001, target: colorado-sb-205); gap_count: 0

### eu-ai-act (26 gap rows)

- direction: reference-beyond-target (source: eu-ai-act, target: iso42001); gap_count: 18
  - Article 43, Annex VI and VII: Conformity assessment procedures. EU AI Act prescribes internal-control and notified-body conformity assessment routes. ISO 42001 is a management-system certification and does not confer or replace EU conformity assessment. [Regulation (EU) 2024/1689]
  - Articles 47-48: CE marking and EU declaration of conformity. CE marking and EU declaration of conformity are regulatory artefacts with no ISO management-system equivalent. [Regulation (EU) 2024/1689]
  - Articles 49 and 71: EU database registration. EU-level high-risk database registration is a public registry obligation absent from ISO 42001. [Regulation (EU) 2024/1689]
  - Article 22: Authorised representative for non-EU providers. EU market-entry representative mandate has no ISO 42001 counterpart. [Regulation (EU) 2024/1689]
  - Article 73: Serious incident reporting deadlines (15/10/2 days). ISO 42001 has no incident-reporting deadline equivalent to Article 73 windows (15 days generic, 10 days widespread infringement, 2 days death). [Regulation (EU) 2024/1689]
  - (+13 more; query crosswalk-matrix-builder for the full list)
- direction: target-beyond-reference (source: iso42001, target: eu-ai-act); gap_count: 8
  - A.2.4: Review of the AI policy. ISO requires periodic AI policy review cadence. EU AI Act does not mandate a policy review cycle; Article 17 QMS obligations imply maintenance but no cadence. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A]
  - A.3.3: Reporting of concerns. ISO mandates internal concern/whistleblower channel. EU AI Act has Article 73 external incident reporting but no internal channel requirement. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A]
  - A.4.2-A.4.6: Resource controls. ISO specifies resource controls across human, tooling, systems, data, and compute. EU Article 17(1)(l) is less granular and does not break out resource categories. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A]
  - A.6.1.4: Impact assessment process documentation. ISO requires documenting the impact-assessment process itself (methodology). EU AI Act Article 27 requires outputs (the FRIA) but not process-level documentation. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A]
  - A.9.3: Objectives for responsible use. ISO requires setting responsible-use objectives. EU Article 26 imposes use obligations but does not require defined use-objectives. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Annex A]
  - (+3 more; query crosswalk-matrix-builder for the full list)

### nist-ai-rmf (11 gap rows)

- direction: reference-beyond-target (source: nist-ai-rmf, target: iso42001); gap_count: 5
  - MEASURE 2.6: AI system safety. ISO 42001 has no dedicated safety-testing control. Safety considerations are distributed across A.6.2.4 (verification and validation) and A.5.4 (impact assessment) without a discrete safety subcategory. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - MEASURE 2.9: Model explained and interpretable. ISO 42001 contains no explicit explainability or interpretability mandate. A.8.2 (system documentation) addresses transparency to users but not model-level explainability. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - MEASURE 2.12: Environmental impact. ISO 42001 contains no control on environmental impact of AI systems. A.5.5 (societal impacts) can be interpreted to include environmental concerns but does not require measurement. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - MEASURE 3.1-3.3: Risk-tracking mechanisms at NIST subcategory granularity. ISO 42001 treats risk tracking under Clause 6.1 (actions to address risks and opportunities) at the management-system level, not at the subcategory granularity NIST specifies in MEASURE 3.1-3.3. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - MEASURE 4.1-4.3: Measurement feedback. ISO 42001 treats measurement feedback under Clause 9 (performance evaluation) at the management-system level. NIST subcategory-level feedback mechanisms have no direct equivalent in Annex A controls. See the paired Clause 9.2 to MEASURE 4.1, 4.2, 4.3 partial-match entries below for the clause-level relationship. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
- direction: target-beyond-reference (source: iso42001, target: nist-ai-rmf); gap_count: 6
  - A.2.4: Review of the AI policy. NIST AI RMF does not mandate a periodic AI policy review cadence. This is a management-system artifact inherited from the ISO 27001 structure. The closest analog is GOVERN 1.7 (decommissioning procedures), which is not equivalent. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - A.4.2: Resources for AI systems. NIST AI RMF contains no resource-provision subcategory. ISO 42001 treats resource identification and allocation as a distinct management-system requirement. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - A.4.4: Tooling resources. NIST AI RMF contains no dedicated tooling-resources subcategory. MAP 4.1 is complementary but scoped to third-party risk rather than internal tooling provision. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - A.4.5: System and computing resources. NIST AI RMF contains no compute-resources subcategory. ISO 42001 treats computing-capacity planning as a distinct management-system control. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - A.6.2.3: Documentation of AI system design (process-level). NIST AI RMF documents controls but not the design process itself. ISO 42001 A.6.2.3 requires process-level documentation of design decisions that NIST subcategories do not specify. [NIST AI 600-1 Appendix A]
  - (+1 more; query crosswalk-matrix-builder for the full list)

### uk-atrs (13 gap rows)

- direction: reference-beyond-target (source: uk-atrs, target: iso42001); gap_count: 6
  - UK ATRS, Section 1.3 Website URL: Public URL (Tier 1). ISO 42001 does not require publication of a public URL for the AI system. ATRS 1.3 is jurisdiction-specific public transparency. [UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard v2.1]
  - UK ATRS, Section 2.8 Public Sector Equality Duty: Equality Act 2010 s.149 integration in impact assessment. ATRS 2.8 expects documentation of Public Sector Equality Duty compliance under Equality Act 2010 s.149. ISO 42001 is jurisdiction-agnostic and does not reference UK statutory equality duties. [UK Equality Act 2010, section 149]
  - UK ATRS, Section 4.1 Senior Responsible Owner: SRO naming under UK GovS 005. ATRS 4.1 expects a named Senior Responsible Owner consistent with UK GovS 005. ISO Clause 5.3 requires defined roles but does not require the SRO title or equivalent UK civil-service accountability construct. [UK Government Functional Standard GovS 005: Digital]
  - UK ATRS, Section Tier 1 public transparency default: CDDO December 2023 mandated public registry. ATRS Tier 1 is a public-registry default mandated by CDDO in December 2023. ISO A.9.3 requires external communication with interested parties but does not require publication to a public registry. [UK CDDO transparency mandate]
  - UK ATRS, Section 2.1 Data Ethics Framework: Data Ethics Framework and ETAF cross-references. ATRS 2.1 expects ethics reasoning referencing UK Data Ethics Framework and ETAF. ISO 42001 references generic AI ethics principles but not these UK-specific frameworks. [UK Data Ethics Framework]
  - (+1 more; query crosswalk-matrix-builder for the full list)
- direction: target-beyond-reference (source: iso42001, target: uk-atrs); gap_count: 7
  - Clauses 4-10: AI Management System organisation-wide requirements. ISO 42001 Clauses 4 to 10 require an organisation-wide management system (context, leadership, planning, support, operation, performance evaluation, improvement). ATRS documents a single tool and does not constitute an AIMS. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023]
  - Clause 9.1: Continuous monitoring and measurement. ISO Clause 9.1 and the NIST MEASURE function both require ongoing measurement with defined metrics. ATRS Tier 1 does not require continuous measurement metrics as a published artefact. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023]
  - A.10.3: Supplier audit rights documentation. ISO A.10.3 expects documented supplier controls including audit rights. ATRS 4.5 asks for supplier name and procurement route but does not require audit-right documentation. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023]
  - A.6.2.2: Model and data card depth. ISO A.6.2.2 and the NIST GenAI Profile anticipate model, data, and system cards with defined depth. ATRS Tier 1 fields are shorter-form and do not mandate full model-card depth. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023]
  - Clause 8: Lifecycle stage mapping. ISO Clause 8 and the NIST AI RMF require AI lifecycle-stage mapping (design, development, deployment, operation, decommissioning). ATRS treats the tool as a static published artefact without lifecycle-stage labelling. [ISO/IEC 42001:2023]
  - (+2 more; query crosswalk-matrix-builder for the full list)
